humanities_role_explanation: Features school graduation exam questions on history and language/literature (content) to evaluate linguistic proficiency and socio-cultural/historical domain knowledge across multiple countries.
benchmark_modalities_explanation: Integrates monolingual multiple-choice text with visual components, utilizing image placeholders within questions or option sets for multimodal evaluation.
analytical_tasks_explanation: Operationalizes factual recall in history and social sciences, reading comprehension in language subjects, causal inference on historical shifts, and multi-step reasoning for science and math problems.

general_purpose_benchmark.note: Constructed from human academic exams across diverse subjects including history, social science, math, natural science, and language.

evaluates_generative_llm.note: Evaluates closed-source models (GPT-4, ChatGPT, Claude) and open-source models (BLOOM, Vicuna) alongside multimodal LLMs (BLIP-2, InstructBLIP, Fromage, OpenFlamingo).

llm_is_primary_system.note: The LLMs/MLLMs are the primary systems being benchmarked.

arts_humanities_data.note: Includes subjects directly mapped to History, Language and Linguistics, and Literature and Literary Theory.

english_language.note: The paper is written in English, and English is one of the nine languages featured in the benchmark.

summary: M3Exam is a multilingual, multimodal, and multilevel benchmark constructed from real-world human graduation exams across nine countries. It contains 12,317 multiple-choice questions spanning three educational levels (primary, middle, and high school), with 23% of questions requiring visual understanding. The benchmark evaluates LLMs on their general intelligence, multilingual capabilities, and multimodal reasoning.
key_findings: GPT-4 is the only model to achieve over 60% average accuracy (scoring 72.92%), outperforming all other models but still struggling with low-resource languages like Javanese and non-Latin scripts like Thai. Multimodal LLMs performed poorly on complex visual questions, with BLIP-2 achieving only 49.06% accuracy. Surprisingly, LLMs do not exhibit a monotonic performance decrease as educational levels increase, indicating their intelligence development differs from humans.
